parentQuestion: Which one of the following systems responds fastest to environmental stimuli?

scaffolds.q: Different body systems respond at different speeds.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Some systems respond in milliseconds, others take hours or days.

scaffolds.q: The NERVOUS system uses ELECTRICAL signals (action potentials).
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Electrical signals travel along nerve fibers at incredible speeds.

scaffolds.q: Nerve signals can travel up to 120 meters per second.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! That's about 270 miles per hour - extremely fast!

scaffolds.q: The ENDOCRINE system uses HORMONES that travel through blood.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Hormones must travel through the bloodstream - slower than nerves.

scaffolds.q: Hormones take seconds to hours to produce effects.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Blood must carry them to target cells, then they trigger responses.

scaffolds.q: The IMMUNE system takes hours to days to mount a full response.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Immune cells need time to recognize threats, multiply, and attack.

scaffolds.q: For FAST responses (milliseconds), the NERVOUS system is unmatched.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Touch a hot stove - you pull your hand back instantly. Nerves!

scaffolds.q: The NERVOUS system responds FASTEST - milliseconds vs. seconds/hours.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Electrical signals beat chemical signals for speed.

scaffolds.q: The answer is NERVOUS system - fastest response to stimuli.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Not immune (days), not muscular (needs nervous input). Nervous = fastest.
